QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_66

question.text: The client-level position limit for futures and options on individual securities is the higher of:

answer.text: 1% of the free-float market capitalization (in number of shares) or 5% of the open interest in all derivative contracts in the same underlying per exchange
5% of the free-float market capitalization or 10% of the open interest
2% of the total market capitalization or 15% of the open interest
There are no client-level position limits for individual securities

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Per SEBI regulations, the client-level position limit for stock derivatives is the higher of: 1% of free-float market capitalization (in shares) or 5% of OI in all derivative contracts on that stock per exchange. This is computed security-wise. **The Trap:** 1% is of FREE-FLOAT market cap, not total market cap. And 5% is of OI, not MWPL. **Cross-Exam Context:** Position limit hierarchy: Client (1%/5%) < TM (20% MWPL) < MWPL (20% free float). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Client stock limit: higher of 1% free float or 5% OI.
